  • Blog
  • Notes

Raised in NY. Applied Math Sc.B. from Brown. Spent some time working in quantitative finance. Currently an (incoming) graduate student interested in probability theory and high dimensional statistics.

You can contact me at timothysudijono@gmail.com.

bio

2021-Current: Ph.D. Statistics, Stanford University.

2019-2021: Quant Portfolio Management, AQR Capital, Greenwich, CT. Equities Long Short.

2015-2019: Sc.B. APMA, Brown University.
     (Honors, Phi Beta Kappa, Rohn Truell Prize)

Summer'18: Intern, AQR Capital, Greenwich, CT. Equities Long Only.

Summer'17: Student Researcher, ICERM, Providence, RI. Topological Data Analysis.

research

At Brown, I did research in probability theory, topological data analysis, and applied bayesian statistics. I was very fortunate to be mentored by Kavita Ramanan, Jose Perea, and Lorin Crawford.

* Denotes co-first authorship.

  1. SINATRA Pro: A Statistical Framework for Identifying Structural Differences in Proteins
    W. S. Tang, G. M. da Silva, H. Kirveslahti, E. Skeens, B. Feng, T. Sudijono, K. Yang, S. Mukherjee, B. Rubinstein, L. Crawford
    In Preparation, 2021.
  2. Periodicity Detection of Sampled Functions via Topological Analysis of Dynamic Networks
    T. Sudijono, C. Tralie, J. Perea
    In Preparation, 2020+.
  3. A Statistical Pipeline for Identifying Physical Features that Differentiate Classes of 3D Shapes
    B. Wang*, T. Sudijono*, H. Kirveslahti* , T. Gao, D. Boyer, S. Mukherjee, L. Crawford
    Accepted at Annals of Applied Statistics, 2020. bioRxiv:701391
  4. Stationarity and Ergodicity of Local Dynamics of Interacting Markov Chains on Large Sparse Graphs
    T. Sudijono (Advised by K. Ramanan, A. Ganguly)
    Sc. B. Thesis - May 2019. bdr:919182 Updated
